title: Coordinates, Scalars, Vector2, Angles, and Units · Runtime Lens: Vector Semantics
summary: The ship stores integer x and y, mixes degrees with radians, normalizes a zero vector, treats screen y as mathematical y, and adds pixels per second directly to position. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the vector semantics increment in unit-labeled 2D geometry model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case prac…
objectives: Use Vector2 addition, subtraction, scaling, magnitude, squared distance, dot, cross, copy, and mutation with explicit meaning.
Declare screen origin, axes, handedness, logical viewport, centers, bounds, and world-to-screen conversion.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Predict Vector Semantics · Theory Vector Semantics
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Asteroids team handles case 9945 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile screen coordinates, Vector2 magnitude and direction, angle convention, and explicit motion units; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-coordinate-vector-units-2
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-coordinate-vector-units-2.
# Case 7417: a headless continuous-integration replay.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def worked_asteroids_coordinate_vector_units_2(x: float = 3.0, y: float = 4.0, pixels_per_second: float = 120.0, seconds: float = 0.25, zero_normalized: bool = False) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "playerworked2",
        "seed7417",
        "input2dekrow",
        "frameworked27417",
        "state7417worked2",
        "playtest2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    magnitude = (x * x + y * y) ** 0.5
    if magnitude <= 0.0:
        return False, magnitude, "zero-vector"
    if pixels_per_second < 0.0 or seconds < 0.0:
        return False, seconds, "unit-domain"
    if zero_normalized:
        return False, magnitude, "zero-normalization"
    displacement = pixels_per_second * seconds
    assert abs(magnitude - 5.0) < 1e-9
    return True, displacement, "explicit-units"

For repair the vector semantics defect, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Vector Semantics model · Theory Vector Semantics
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Vector Semantics: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 1659 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile screen coordinates, Vector2 magnitude and direction, angle convention, and explicit motion units; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Use Vector2 addition, subtraction, scaling, magnitude, squared distance, dot, cross, copy, and mutation with explicit meaning.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Vector Semantics model · Theory Vector Semantics.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Vector Semantics model · Theory Vector Semantics, keep Vector Semantics connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use Vector2 addition, subtraction, scaling, magnitude, squared distance, dot, cross, copy, and mutation with explicit meaning.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Vector Semantics model · Theory Vector Semantics, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Vector Semantics precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Vector Semantics model · Theory Vector Semantics, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 1659 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile screen coordinates, Vector2 magnitude and direction, angle convention, and explicit motion units; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Use Vector2 addition, subtraction, scaling, magnitude, squared distance, dot, cross, copy, and mutation with explicit meaning.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Vector Semantics model · Theory Vector Semantics, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Every Vector2 operation returns a new value and cannot alias state.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: asteroids-vector-semantics-rcvp8j(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_asteroids_vector_semantics_rcvp8j\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?magnitude <= 0.0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?pixels_per_second < 0.0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?zero_normalized)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?pixels_per_second \* seconds)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
